name: Baked Snapper Gremolata
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: Dish may also be prepared with cod, flounder, tilefish, turbot, or even oily fish such as bluefish, mako shark, or seatrout. Heat oven to 400 degrees.
In a large bowl, toss potatoes with olive oil, 1 teaspoon of fresh thyme (or 1/2 teaspoon dried), a pinch of salt, and a grinding of pepper. Spread the mixture in an even layer on a jelly roll pan measuring 15x10 inches. Roast until the potatoes are browned, about 35 minutes.
Remove the pan from the oven and increase the temperature to 450 degrees. In a small bowl, blend parsley, lemon zest, and remaining thyme. Sprinkle lemon juice over fish fillets, then sprinkle lightly with salt and pepper. Arrange the fish on top of the potatoes. Return the pan to the oven and bake until the fish is opaque in the center, about 10 minutes.
For each individual serving, scoop up crusty potatoes topped with fish. Garnish with fresh parsley sprigs if desired.
